summary: Observed implementation only: current-user Resume setting under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CrossDeviceResume\Configuration\IsResumeAllowed. Repo Procmon notes show Settings > Apps > Resume writing 0 when the feature is turned off and 1 when it is turned on. This record intentionally models that narrower user preference, not the broader official machine policy for Phone-PC linking and Continue on PC experiences. The setting is useful as a real runtime surface, but it is not yet backed by a primary Microsoft source.

setting.name: Disable Resume Experiences
setting.casual_explanation: Resume lets you start on one device and continue on this PC. This tweak targets the current-user Resume setting that Settings exposes, so it controls the handoff experience for the signed-in user rather than pushing a device-wide policy.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it off when they do not want cross-device handoff behavior for their own account. They leave it on when they use Continue on PC or related app handoff flows.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces cross-device convenience for the current user. This record stays in review because the user setting is runtime-observed rather than directly documented by a primary Microsoft source.
setting.professional_notes: Repo Procmon notes in Docs/privacy/privacy.md show Settings > Apps > Resume writing HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CrossDeviceResume\Configuration\IsResumeAllowed = 0 when Resume is turned off and 1 when Resume is turned on. The same note says stock Windows 11 25H2 defaults Resume to enabled and uses companion values such as IsOneDriveResumeAllowed for app-specific participation. This is narrower than the official machine policy already covered by privacy.disable-phone-linking, which uses EnableMmx and blocks broader Phone-PC linking and Continue on PC participation. This record therefore documents the observed current-user Resume surface the app actually writes and keeps it in docs-first territory because it is not yet backed by a primary Microsoft source.

allowed_values.label: No explicit user setting
allowed_values.meaning: Windows uses its default Resume behavior for the current user. Repo notes say stock Windows 11 25H2 defaults Resume to enabled, but that default is not yet backed by a primary Microsoft source.

allowed_values.label: Resume disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Observed Settings and app value for turning Resume off for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Resume enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Observed Settings value for turning Resume on for the current user.

targets.notes: This record models the narrower current-user Resume toggle only. The broader machine policy for Phone-PC linking and Continue on PC is tracked separately by privacy.disable-phone-linking via EnableMmx.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes IsResumeAllowed = 0 under the observed current-user CrossDeviceResume setting. That matches the runtime surface this record intentionally documents.

windows_defaults.label: Observed Windows baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Current-user profiles using Resume on Windows 11

states.rationale: Leave the user setting unset and let Windows keep its ordinary Resume behavior.

windows_defaults.notes: Repo Procmon notes say stock Windows 11 25H2 defaults Resume to enabled, but this remains an observed baseline rather than a primary-source-backed default.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want Windows to keep its normal Resume behavior
avoid_for: People who explicitly want Resume off for the current user

states.rationale: Leave the observed user setting unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its ordinary cross-device Resume behavior for the current user.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to explicitly disable Resume for the current user account.
tradeoffs: Resume stays available where Windows would normally allow it.
The app avoids publishing a runtime-observed setting as fully validated policy.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable Resume for current user
intended_for: People who want the Settings > Apps > Resume toggle off for the signed-in user
avoid_for: Anyone expecting a broader device-wide Phone-PC linking block

states.rationale: Observed Settings and app value for turning Resume off for the current user.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the narrower per-user Resume preference off without relying on the broader machine policy.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a fully documented Microsoft policy surface or a device-wide block.
tradeoffs: Disables Resume for the current user account.
This remains not yet backed by a primary Microsoft source and is therefore kept as docs-first research.
recommended_profiles.notes: This is kept as docs-first because the setting is runtime-observed and not yet backed by a primary Microsoft source.

evidence.title: Repo Procmon notes for Resume settings
evidence.summary: Repo notes show Settings > Apps > Resume writing IsResumeAllowed = 0 when Resume is off and IsResumeAllowed = 1 when Resume is on. The same notes say stock Windows 11 25H2 defaults Resume to enabled and uses companion values such as IsOneDriveResumeAllowed.

evidence.title: Local CrossDeviceResume registry observation
evidence.summary: Local read-only registry query on 2026-03-13 found IsResumeAllowed = 0 and IsOneDriveResumeAllowed = 1 under the current-user CrossDeviceResume configuration key, confirming the observed path is present and active on this build.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CrossDeviceResume\Configuration\IsResumeAllowed = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft GroupPolicy.admx / GroupPolicy.adml context for Phone-PC linking
evidence.summary: The official machine policy EnableMmx blocks broader Phone-PC linking and Continue on PC experiences. This record uses that context only to explain scope boundaries and why privacy.disable-phone-linking remains a separate, broader control.

decision.why: This record intentionally documents a real runtime user setting the app actually writes, but it is observed implementation only and not yet backed by a primary Microsoft source. The broader official machine policy already exists separately as privacy.disable-phone-linking.
blocking_issues: IsResumeAllowed is not yet backed by a primary Microsoft source. Current semantics come from repo Procmon notes and local observation rather than official Microsoft documentation.

open_questions: Can a primary Microsoft source be found for HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CrossDeviceResume\Configuration\IsResumeAllowed?

Should this remain as a narrow current-user Resume toggle, or should the product steer users toward the broader privacy.disable-phone-linking policy when they want a fully documented Microsoft-managed surface?
